name: Fight Colorectal Cancer
mission: Learn what colorectal cancer is, how it develops, and the key facts, symptoms, and screening guidelines to know.
theoryOfChange: Fight Colorectal Cancer has a published strategic framework called "Our Path to a Cure," which serves as a visionary roadmap for their research advocacy and strategic planning. This document outlines goals, priorities, and the evidence guiding their approach to improve colorectal cancer prevention, detection, treatment, and survivorship. The organization publishes an annual Progress Report that scores indicators for each pillar of this framework, demonstrating accountability and tracking advancements. Additionally, their "Colorectal Cancer Care Initiative" is a national coalition focused on data-driven, actionable goals to transform colorectal cancer screening, diagnosis, and treatment, providing a tool to measure progress and improve outcomes.
scoreSummary: Fight Colorectal Cancer shows strong impact and good alignment (minor risk deductions applied).

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Good donor alignment with manageable risk. Evidence quality is solid but still evolving in places.

keyConcerns.headline: 100% of expenses stay in the US despite international operations

keyConcerns.detail: For orgs with revenue <$5M, CEO pay above 5% of revenue warrants scrutiny.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization earns a 61/100 due to its high financial efficiency and strong operational health, though its alignment score is moderated by a lower specific fit for traditional Muslim donor priorities.

dimension_explanations.alignment: While the cause addresses urgent global health needs, the alignment for Muslim donors is considered low as there are no specific programs tailored to this community [8]. As a Sadaqah-eligible organization, it provides a pathway for general voluntary charitable giving toward medical advocacy [8].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization demonstrates high financial health and effectiveness by maintaining a program expense ratio of 85.2% [1][2]. Its focus on capacity building and public policy aims to create systemic change in global health, supported by a robust 12.0 months of working capital [5].

baseline_narrative.headline: Fight Colorectal Cancer empowers a global community to advance policy, research, and patient education in the fight against colon and rectal cancers.
strengths: High program expense ratio of 85.2% ensures the vast majority of funds reach core advocacy and research programs.
Exceptional fundraising efficiency with a cost of only $0.07 per $1 raised.
Strong liquidity position with 12.0 months of working capital available.
baseline_narrative.summary: Fight Colorectal Cancer is a national advocacy organization dedicated to supporting patients and champions through public policy initiatives and medical research [1][2]. In the most recent fiscal period, the organization reported a total revenue of $3,060,301 to fund its mission of awareness and patient empowerment [2][5].

areas_for_improvement.context: While the organization tracks outcomes, it currently only reports <cite id="11">one specific outcome metric via Candid</cite>, making cost-per-beneficiary analysis difficult.

For an organization with approximately $3M in revenue, the <cite id="2">CEO compensation of $161,283</cite> represents over 5% of total revenue, which may warrant closer donor scrutiny regarding governance.

credibility.explanation: Donors can trust this charity due to its <cite id="11">Platinum Seal of Transparency</cite> and the fact that it <cite id="11">meets all 20 BBB Wise Giving Alliance Standards</cite>. It also maintains a <cite id="1">100% independent board</cite>.
credibility.improvement: To further strengthen credibility, the organization could publish more detailed <cite id="11">annual impact reports</cite> that include specific beneficiary counts.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects a <cite id="11">verified theory of change</cite> and outcome tracking, though rigorous external evaluations like RCTs are not present.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: Fight CRC uses advocacy and research funding to influence public policy and accelerate medical breakthroughs for colorectal cancer patients.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that advocacy efforts are translating into actual policy improvements and research progress.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: With a <cite id="2">$3.1M revenue base</cite> and a significant funding gap in cancer advocacy, the organization has high capacity to absorb additional capital.

peer_comparison.differentiator: Distinguished by a <cite id="1">fundraising cost of only $0.07 per dollar</cite>, which is significantly lower than many peers.

similar_organizations.differentiator: Similar focus on advocacy and research policy
similar_organizations.name: Heart Women & Girls

strengths.detail: The organization directs <cite id="1">83.4% of its total expenses</cite> toward its core programs in advocacy and research, exceeding the <cite id="1">peer program ratio median of 80.0%</cite>.
Fight CRC maintains a highly lean fundraising operation, requiring only <cite id="1">$0.07 in expenses for every $1 raised</cite>.
The charity maintains <cite id="1">12.0 months of working capital</cite> in reserve, providing a significant buffer for long-term project stability.

strengths_deep_dive: Fight CRC's operational strength is rooted in its ability to maintain high transparency and accountability standards, as evidenced by its <cite id="11">Platinum Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite> and its status as a <cite id="11">BBB Accredited Charity</cite> meeting all 20 standards for accountability. Financially, the organization has shown steady growth with a <cite id="2">3-year revenue CAGR of 10.9%</cite>, growing from $2.48 million in 2021 to over $3.06 million in 2023. This growth supports a robust grantmaking portfolio that includes <cite id="2">$178,973 in domestic grants</cite> across seven recipients, focusing on high-impact research and policy collaboration.
rich_narrative.summary: Fight Colorectal Cancer (Fight CRC) is a national advocacy and research organization that empowers a global community to advance policy, research, and patient education. The organization reported <cite id="2">$3,060,301 in total revenue</cite> for the 2023 fiscal year, primarily driven by <cite id="2">$2,946,341 in contributions</cite>. Operating as a capacity-building entity, Fight CRC focuses on <cite id="8">advocacy, research, and patient support</cite> to improve outcomes for those affected by colon and rectal cancers. Their financial model is characterized by high efficiency, maintaining a <cite id="1">program expense ratio of 85.2%</cite>, which ensures that the vast majority of donor funds are directed toward core mission activities rather than administrative overhead. Furthermore, the organization demonstrates exceptional fiscal discipline, spending only <cite id="1">$0.07 to raise every $1</cite> in charitable contributions. Beyond direct advocacy, the organization acts as a strategic funder, providing <cite id="2">$186,573 in total grants</cite> to partners such as the <cite id="2">American Cancer Society</cite> to further collaborative research and support initiatives.
